overview.tagline: A charming village with tree-lined streets, top-rated schools, and a vibrant downtown
overview.description: The Village of Ridgewood is one of Bergen County's most desirable communities, known for its walkable downtown filled with boutique shops and restaurants, excellent public schools consistently ranked among New Jersey's best, and beautiful historic homes. The village combines small-town charm with easy access to New York City, making it a premier destination for families and professionals.
highlights: Award-winning downtown shopping and dining district
Top-rated public schools in New Jersey
NJ Transit rail service to Hoboken and NYC

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Ridgewood conducted a municipal-wide revaluation in recent years to align assessed values with market values.
realEstate.recentTrends: Ridgewood's real estate market remains highly competitive with strong demand, particularly for homes near the downtown and train station. Prices have appreciated steadily, and homes often receive multiple offers. The market favors sellers, with limited inventory driving prices upward.
neighborhoodNotes: The Heights — Elevated neighborhood with larger lots and scenic views
Ridgewood Avenue corridor — Walking distance to downtown shops and restaurants
Upper Ridgewood — Spacious properties near the country club
Glen Avenue area — Family-friendly streets close to elementary schools
West Side — Charming homes with easy access to Paramus shopping

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided weekly by the Village of Ridgewood Department of Public Works.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days by neighborhood zone
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids, maximum 50 lbs per container
Bulk item pickup available by appointment
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on village website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream curbside recycling is collected weekly by the Village.
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastic #1-#7) collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected separately on the same day
Recycling bins provided by the Village
Yard waste collected seasonally from April through December
Electronics recycling events held periodically at the DPW yard

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Ridgewood Water is one of New Jersey's oldest and most respected public water utilities, serving the village and neighboring communities.
details: Ridgewood Water supplies water from local wells and the Oradell Reservoir system
Billing is quarterly for water and sewer service
Water quality reports published annually and available online
Sewer service is maintained by the Village DPW
Emergency water line repairs available 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning approvals, and code enforcement for the Village.
details: Building permits required for new construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Board of Adjustment
Permit applications available online and in person
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Historic Preservation Commission review required for designated properties

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Village operates several municipal parking lots and metered street parking in the downtown area.
details: Commuter parking permits available for train station lots
Metered parking in downtown — 2-hour limit in most zones
Municipal lots on Garber Square, Hudson Street, and North Broad Street
ParkMobile app accepted at all metered spaces
Overnight parking restrictions enforced village-wide

additionalServices.title: Ridgewood Public Library
additionalServices.description: Full-service public library offering books, digital resources, programs, and community meeting spaces.
details: Located at 125 North Maple Avenue
Children's programs, teen events, and adult book clubs
Free Wi-Fi, computer access, and 3D printing
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues, swim programs, and summer camps
Adult fitness classes and senior programs
Schedler House and Kasschau Shell bandshell events
Online registration through Community Pass

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: The Village of Ridgewood enforces noise regulations to maintain quality of life in residential neighborhoods.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 6:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers restricted to certain hours and seasons
Amplified music and loud gatherings subject to noise limits after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners are required to maintain their properties in good condition, including lawns, structures, and sidewalks.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept under 10 inches
Property owners responsible for sidewalk snow removal within 24 hours after snowfall
Fences must not exceed 6 feet in rear yards and 4 feet in front yards
Abandoned or inoperable vehicles must be removed from property
Trees overhanging public sidewalks must be trimmed to 8 feet clearance

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Ridgewood requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws throughout the village.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately by the owner
Maximum of 3 dogs and 3 cats per household
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the village with specific rules for residential areas and the downtown.
keyRules: No overnight parking on village streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM without a permit
2-hour parking limit in downtown metered zones
Snow emergency parking bans enforced — vehicles must be off-street
Commercial vehicles over 4 tons prohibited from parking on residential streets
Handicapped parking violations subject to significant fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties in Ridgewood must be registered and inspected to ensure compliance with housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Village
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy of rental units
Landlords must maintain properties to housing code standards
Short-term rentals (under 30 days) are not permitted in residential zones
Rental property inspections conducted at change of tenancy

other.title: Tree Preservation
other.summary: Ridgewood has a strong tree preservation ordinance protecting significant trees on public and private property.
keyRules: Permit required to remove trees over 8 inches in diameter on private property
Replacement planting may be required when trees are removed
Village Shade Tree Committee oversees public tree management
Damage to public trees subject to fines and restitution

other.title: Outdoor Dining & Sidewalk Use
other.summary: Restaurants may apply for outdoor dining permits in the downtown area, subject to conditions.
keyRules: Outdoor dining permits issued seasonally by the Village
Minimum sidewalk clearance of 5 feet must be maintained
Alcohol service in outdoor areas requires separate ABC approval
Furniture must be removed or secured nightly

publicSchools.name: Ridgewood High School
publicSchools.notes: Consistently ranked among top high schools in New Jersey; strong AP program and athletics

publicSchools.name: Benjamin Franklin Middle School
publicSchools.notes: One of two middle schools serving the village

publicSchools.name: George Washington Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive middle school with strong academics and extracurriculars

publicSchools.name: Hawes Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Neighborhood elementary school in the Heights section

publicSchools.name: Orchard Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Located in central Ridgewood near the downtown

publicSchools.name: Ridge Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the western portion of the village

publicSchools.name: Somerville Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school on the east side of Ridgewood

publicSchools.name: Willard Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school in the Upper Ridgewood area

privateSchools.name: Bergen County Academies
privateSchools.notes: Bergen County magnet school located in Hackensack; many Ridgewood students apply

privateSchools.name: Our Lady of Mount Carmel School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic parish school in downtown Ridgewood

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 15 minutes

whyMoveHere: Top-rated public school district consistently ranked in New Jersey's top 10
Walkable, charming downtown with independent shops, restaurants, and cafes
Direct NJ Transit train service to Hoboken Terminal with connections to NYC
Beautiful tree-lined streets with a mix of historic and modern homes
Strong sense of community with active civic organizations and events
Low crime rate and family-friendly neighborhoods

commuteToNYC.duration: 45-60 minutes to Hoboken, then PATH or ferry to Manhattan
commuteToNYC.notes: The Ridgewood train station is centrally located in the downtown. Trains run frequently during rush hours. Bus 148 provides express service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Manhattan with a commute of approximately 50-70 minutes depending on traffic.

costOfLiving.summary: Ridgewood is an affluent community with a cost of living significantly above state and national averages, driven primarily by housing costs and property taxes.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 55% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Exceptional public schools from elementary through high school
Vibrant, walkable downtown with dining, shopping, and entertainment
Strong property values with consistent appreciation
Excellent community amenities including parks, pool, and recreation programs
Convenient train access to New York City
cons: Very high property taxes, among the highest in the state
Home prices are well above regional averages
Train commute to Manhattan requires a transfer at Hoboken
Downtown parking can be challenging, especially on weekends
Limited housing inventory keeps prices elevated

restaurants.name: Latour
restaurants.description: Upscale French restaurant offering refined cuisine in an elegant setting. A Ridgewood fine dining institution for decades.

restaurants.name: It's Greek To Me
restaurants.description: Popular Greek restaurant known for fresh seafood, traditional dishes, and a lively atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Cafe Amici
restaurants.description: Italian-American restaurant and bar offering classic pasta dishes, pizzas, and a warm neighborhood vibe.

restaurants.name: Dim Sum Dynasty
restaurants.description: Authentic Chinese restaurant serving dim sum and traditional Cantonese cuisine in downtown Ridgewood.

restaurants.name: Country Pancake House
restaurants.description: Beloved breakfast and brunch spot famous for creative pancakes, omelets, and a cozy atmosphere.

parks.name: Graydon Pool & Park
parks.description: A scenic natural swimming area and park set around a spring-fed pond. A beloved summer destination for Ridgewood families with swimming, sunbathing, and picnicking.

parks.name: Veteran's Memorial Park
parks.description: Central park with walking paths, memorial installations, and open green space in the heart of the village.
parks.address: East Ridgewood Avenue at Broad Street, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

parks.name: Habernickel Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park featuring playgrounds, sports fields, and picnic areas popular with families.

parks.name: Schedler Park & Kasschau Shell
parks.description: Historic bandshell and park hosting summer concerts, community events, and performances throughout the year.

events.name: Ridgewood 4th of July Celebration
events.description: Annual Independence Day festivities including a parade through downtown, live music, family activities, and fireworks.

events.name: Ridgewood Fall Festival & Street Fair
events.description: Large street fair with vendors, food, live entertainment, and activities spanning the downtown area.
events.when: October, typically the first or second Sunday

events.name: Summer Concert Series at Kasschau Shell
events.description: Free outdoor concerts held weekly at the Kasschau Shell bandshell in Schedler Park throughout the summer.

events.name: Ridgewood Guild International Food Festival
events.description: Popular annual food festival showcasing cuisines from around the world, hosted by the Ridgewood Guild.
events.when: June, typically a Saturday in mid-June

attractions.name: Downtown Ridgewood
attractions.description: Walkable downtown shopping district featuring over 200 independent boutiques, specialty shops, restaurants, and cafes along East Ridgewood Avenue and side streets.

attractions.name: Ridgewood Public Library
attractions.description: Beautiful public library offering extensive collections, community programs, and a welcoming space for all ages.

attractions.name: The Stable at Ridgewood
attractions.description: Community arts center hosting art exhibits, classes, and cultural events in a historic converted stable building.

shoppingAreas: East Ridgewood Avenue — Main downtown shopping corridor
North Broad Street — Boutiques and specialty shops
Franklin Avenue — Restaurants and small shops
South Broad Street — Additional retail and services
Westwood/Paramus shopping — Major retail centers nearby

government.meetingSchedule: Village Council meets the 2nd and 4th Wednesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Village Hall, 131 North Maple Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
